Home Etc

Mingi Lee

Iâ€™m a Ph.D. student in the 3D Vision Lab at Seoul National University, advised by Prof. Young Min Kim.

My research lies at the intersection of 3D CAD generation and geometric learning, with a focus on bridging functional design and generative modeling.

I aim to develop structure-aware, editable CAD systems that are grounded in real-world usability, by integrating geometric reasoning, differentiable physics, and modern generative techniques.

Beyond research, I love to boulderđź§—, skiâ›·, snorkelđź¤ż, chill with good musicđźŽ¶, and wander for any new adventuresđź—ş.

Feel free to reach out to me for any kinds of discussions or collaborations from research to a climbing session.

Publications

BrepDiff: Single-stage B-rep Diffusion Model

Mingi Lee*, Dongsu Zhang*, ClĂ©ment Jambon*, Young Min Kim

SIGGRAPH 2025

BrepDiff is a simple, single-stage diffusion model for generating Boundary Representations (B-reps). It generates B-reps by denoising point-based face samples with a dedicated noise schedule. Unlike multi-stage methods, BrepDiff enables intuitive, editable geometry creation, including completion, merging, and interpolation, while achieving competitive performance on unconditional generation.
